competitor_patterns: Top-ranking content is extremely long-form (6,000–24,000 words), functioning as comprehensive buyer guides rather than single-product articles — Bikerumor leads at 24,354 words testing 18 products

Competitors consistently use comparison tables with spec-by-spec breakdowns (weight, lens type, UV rating, price) — seen in Bikerumor, road.cc, and 220triathlon — making content scannable and decision-ready
All top-3 pages include FAQ sections (Bikerumor, Cyclist) targeting voice search and Google's 'People Also Ask' boxes, typically 6–10 questions covering fit, lens choice, and price justification
Freshness signals are prominent: Bikerumor explicitly states 'updated May 21, 2026' in snippet — regular content refreshes with new product additions drive repeat crawling and SERP trust badges

Competitor H2 structure leads with specific product names (Julbo Density, Oakley Sutro Lite, POC Devour) rather than generic headers, capturing long-tail brand+model search queries within the same page
our_gaps: Our posts average 1,100–3,000 words — dramatically shorter than the 6,000–24,000 word benchmark set by ranking competitors; we lack the content depth Google rewards for high-competition buying-intent queries

No comparison tables with weight/lens/UV/price columns in most posts (only 1 of 5 recent posts has a table) — competitors use tables on nearly every ranking page, improving dwell time and featured snippet eligibility

We target branded/value comparison angles (Alibaba vs Velluto, Bing vs Velluto) but lack pure category-entry content targeting unbranded high-volume queries like 'best cycling glasses 2026' or 'road cycling glasses review' where competitors dominate

Zero third-party review signals embedded in our content — top competitors explicitly cite test methodology ('we tested 18 pairs'), reviewer credentials, and real-world conditions; our posts lack E-E-A-T trust builders

Our posts do not rank for interchangeable lens queries despite this being Velluto's primary technical differentiator — Bergfreunde holds position 1 for '[EN] interchangeable lens cycling glasses' and we have no dedicated optimised page for this keyword cluster
keyword_opportunities: interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Bergfreunde ranks #1 with a category page; Velluto has the strongest product story here (click-in, tool-free, two lens types) but zero dedicated content targeting this exact phrase

lightweight cycling glasses under 30g — no competitor content specifically targets sub-30g weight as a buying criterion; Velluto's 25g frame is a concrete, verifiable spec advantage that could anchor a dedicated post

anti-fog cycling glasses road riding — appears in buyer guides as a feature mention but no standalone article owns this; pairs naturally with Velluto's built-in anti-fog system and the use-case of cold morning rides

cycling glasses 30 day trial risk free — zero competitor content targets this transactional trust query; Velluto's unique 30-day trial is a direct conversion lever and could capture bottom-funnel searchers comparing purchase confidence

best cycling glasses for prescription wearers — Velo.outsideonline ranks #1 for prescription cycling sunglasses review with 12,776 words; Velluto's adjustable nose pads and lens system are relevant to this audience and the keyword cluster has clear intent
next_post_guidelines: Write a 6,000–8,000 word comprehensive buyer guide titled 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Tested & Ranked' — include at least 8 products reviewed with individual H2s per product (use competitor brand names as H2s to capture long-tail model queries), a master comparison table, and a FAQ section of at least 8 questions; position Velluto StradaPro as the 'Best Value Pick' with its own dedicated section

Every post must include at least one HTML comparison table with columns: Weight (g), Lens System, UV Rating, Anti-Fog, Trial Period, Price — this directly targets featured snippet eligibility and matches the table format used by Bikerumor and 220triathlon

Add an explicit 'How We Tested' or 'Why Trust This Review' section (minimum 150 words) citing specific ride conditions, distances, and weather tested — this is the single most visible E-E-A-T signal missing from all current Velluto posts

Target the keyword cluster 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' with a dedicated 2,500+ word post structured around: why interchangeable lenses matter → lens types explained (tinted, clear, high-contrast) → how Velluto's click-in system compares to screw-in competitors → FAQ; use 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' in H1, first 100 words, and at least 3 H2s

Update all existing posts to add a 'Last Updated: June 2026' date stamp visible in the page body and meta snippet — Bikerumor's snippet explicitly shows freshness ('2 weeks ago') which improves CTR; this is a 5-minute win per post

Structure FAQ sections to directly answer 'People Also Ask' questions visible for target queries — minimum questions to include: 'Are expensive cycling glasses worth it?', 'What weight should cycling glasses be?', 'Do interchangeable lenses fit all frames?', 'Can I try cycling glasses before buying?' — each answer should be 40–60 words, self-contained, and include the question keyword

For comparison posts, always lead the H1 and meta title with the category keyword before the brand name (e.g., 'Best Lightweight Cycling Glasses 2026: Velluto vs Oakley' not 'Velluto vs Oakley Comparison') — category-first titles capture broader search volume while the brand names satisfy comparison intent
seo_quick_wins: Add a sticky comparison table in the first 500 words of every post — Google uses the first table it encounters for featured snippets; even a simple 3-column table (Feature | StradaPro | Typical Competitor) with weight, lens system, UV rating, and price increases snippet eligibility immediately

Implement FAQ schema markup (JSON-LD) on all posts that already have FAQ sections — our 4 recent posts with FAQs are currently unstructured HTML; adding schema costs zero content effort and directly enables rich results in Google's 'People Also Ask' for queries we're already targeting

Add internal links from every existing post to a single anchor page targeting 'interchangeable lens cycling glasses' — this consolidates link equity toward our strongest product differentiator keyword and accelerates ranking for a query where Velluto has a genuine product advantage over Bergfreunde (who sells third-party products vs Velluto's own system)

geo_angles: Structure one post as a direct answer to the query 'What is the lightest cycling glasses available in 2026?' with the answer in the first 40 words: 'The Velluto StradaPro weighs 25g, making it one of the lightest cycling glasses available in 2026 — lighter than Oakley Sutro Lite (36g) and POC Devour (28g)' — AI systems including Perplexity and Google AI Overview prioritise concise factual answers with specific numerical claims that can be verified and cited

Create a structured 'Cycling Glasses Comparison Hub' page with a large schema-marked table comparing 10+ glasses on weight, UV rating, lens system, anti-fog, and trial policy — AI answer engines (ChatGPT with browsing, Perplexity) frequently pull comparison data from structured tables when users ask 'compare cycling glasses'; being the most comprehensive structured comparison source increases citation probability

Publish a dedicated page targeting 'cycling glasses with free trial' or 'try cycling glasses before buying' — this is a zero-competition informational angle where Velluto has a unique factual claim (30-day risk-free trial); when users ask ChatGPT or Perplexity 'can I try cycling glasses before buying?', a page that directly answers this with Velluto as the primary example will be cited as the specific answer, especially if it includes schema markup and a clear brand name
comparison_post_angles: BIKERUMOR (as proxy for Oakley Sutro Lite which ranks in their #1 guide): angle — 'Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro: Is the €200 Premium Worth It in 2026?' — headline idea: 'We Wore Both for 500km: Here's What Oakley's Price Tag Actually Buys You' — key differentiator: weight (Oakley Sutro Lite 36g vs StradaPro 25g) and lens system (Oakley's Prizm lenses are single-purchase vs StradaPro's included dual-lens click-in system) — Velluto advantage to lead with: 11g lighter frame + two lenses included + 30-day trial vs Oakley's no-trial, single-lens purchase

BERGFREUNDE (ranks #1 for interchangeable lens cycling glasses): angle — 'Interchangeable Lens Cycling Glasses: What Retailers Don't Tell You About Compatibility' — headline idea: 'Why Buying Interchangeable Lenses From a Multi-Brand Retailer Costs More Than You Think' — key differentiator: Bergfreunde sells third-party glasses where replacement lenses are brand-specific, separately purchased, and often out of stock; Velluto ships both VellutoPuro and VellutoVisione lenses in the box — Velluto advantage to lead with: complete lens system included at purchase, tool-free click-in swap, both lenses optimised for the same frame geometry

VELO.OUTSIDEONLINE (ranks #1 for road cycling glasses review / prescription focus): angle — 'Road Cycling Glasses for Riders Who've Tried Everything: A 2026 Honest Review' — headline idea: 'What 500km of Testing Taught Us About What Road Cyclists Actually Need From Their Glasses' — key differentiator: outsideonline focuses on prescription options (a niche); Velluto can own the 'non-prescription road cyclist who wants performance without Oakley pricing' majority segment — Velluto advantage to lead with: 25g ultra-light build reduces pressure-point fatigue on long rides, anti-fog system for climb-to-descent transitions, Italian design credibility at accessible price point
